276.reason: [The condition for detecting single '|' is incorrect: it should trigger an error when '|' is NOT followed by another '|', but the current logic only triggers an error when it IS followed by '|'. This causes the error detection for single '|' to be missed, and incorrectly flags valid '||' as an error.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition for detecting single '|' is incorrect: it should trigger an error when '|' is NOT followed by another '|', but the current logic only triggers an error when it IS followed by '|'. This causes the error detection for single '|' to be missed, and incorrectly flags valid '||' as an error.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition for detecting single '|' is incorrect: it only triggers an error when peekChar() == '|', which is actually the valid '||' case. It should trigger an error when peekChar() != '|'. Similarly, the '&' part is correct, but the '|' part is inverted.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition for '|' is incorrect: it should trigger an error when '|' is NOT followed by another '|', but the current code only triggers an error when it IS followed by '|'. This logic is inverted for the '|' case.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition for detecting single '|' is incorrect: it should trigger an error when '|' is NOT followed by another '|', but the current logic only triggers when it IS followed by '|'. This causes single '|' to be ignored and single '&' to be (partially) handled, but the logic is inconsistent and misses the case of lone '|'.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 1.0 , reason:  [The condition for '|' is incorrect: it should trigger an error when '|' is NOT followed by another '|', but the current code only triggers error when it IS followed by '|'. This logic is inverted for the '|' case.]
